fragility_points: A temporary dip at the exact settlement minute can flip the market even if BTC is strong most of the week.
Threshold is strict: above 70000, not equal to 70000.
Confidence is sensitive to short-horizon crypto volatility as the date approaches.
key_assumptions: BTC remains above 70000 on Binance into the April 20 12:00 ET settlement minute.
No sharp macro or crypto-specific selloff erases the current roughly 4k cushion.
Binance settlement surface remains straightforward to interpret.
main_logical_chain: Polymarket rules make Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute close the governing source.
BTC is currently around 74k on Binance, giving a roughly 5.7% cushion above the threshold.
That makes Yes the base case, but exact-minute close risk still matters over five remaining days.
Therefore the market direction looks right, but high-80s confidence is slightly rich.
main_thesis: Yes is still the base case because BTC is already trading around 74k on Binance, but the market looks slightly too confident for a single exact-minute close-above contract five days out.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified.
source_quality_view: High quality for mechanics from Polymarket rules; good venue-specific context from Binance; moderate independence from CoinGecko and Coinbase cross-checks.
strongest_disconfirmers: This is a single exact-minute close contract, not a touch market.
A 5-6% BTC move over five days is plausible, so the cushion is meaningful but not decisive.
Only Binance BTC/USDT counts, so wrong-venue comfort would be false confidence.
strongest_supports: Current Binance BTCUSDT spot during the run was 73999.38, already materially above 70000.
Recent Binance 1-minute candles were clustered around 73931-73999 with highs above 74027.
CoinGecko and Coinbase cross-checks were directionally consistent around 74k.
timing_relevance: Timing is central because the contract resolves on one exact Binance 1-minute close at 12:00 ET on April 20.
unresolved_ambiguities: No direct evidence yet exists for the decisive April 20 noon close because the event is still in the future.
I did not independently model expected BTC volatility into the settlement window.
what_would_change_view: I would move down if BTC loses the cushion and trades back near 70k before settlement; I would move up if Binance still shows a comfortable margin closer to April 20.
